original_abstract: Enantioselective allylation is a highly used organic reaction to prepare chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines, which serve as important building blocks in the synthesis of a variety of natural products and pharmaceuticals.
humanized_abstract: Enantioselective allylation is a widely used organic transformation in the synthesis of chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines. The resulting heterocyclic and nonheterocyclic chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines have been incorporated into the synthesis of many natural products and pharmaceuticals.

document.output: Enantioselective allylation is a widely used organic transformation in the synthesis of chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines. The resulting heterocyclic and nonheterocyclic chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines have been incorporated into the synthesis of many natural products and pharmaceuticals.
